prompt: Please do the task stated in `/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2_generation_prompts.md`, where the TASK_ID is `fc4aaf52`.
codex_response:
Rule implemented: swap the two non-background colors, split the single connected figure across its horizontal midpoint, keep the lower half fixed, and shift the upper half right by the seam-row width. I used the Opus hint as a starting check and rejected the Sonnet hint because its claimed rotation does not occur; that mismatch is documented in [notes.md](/home/aaaaa/projects/codex_batch/re-arc/arc2/tasks/task_fc4aaf52/notes.md). No new primitive was needed.

The generator builds the same task family constructively on the fixed `16x16` cyan background: one connected two-color figure made from vertically mirrored horizontal segments, then derives the output by swapping colors and shifting only the upper half. `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.verify --task fc4aaf52` passed `3/3` official examples, and `conda run --no-capture-output -n arc python -m arc2.shared.build --task fc4aaf52 --n-examples 1000` produced `1000` verified examples. The generated preview sheet looked on-family.
